input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-24T00:54:05.912Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to supply light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

This reacts automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` in code-behind:

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();

    // For any BindableProperty:
    this.SetAppTheme(
        ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
        Colors.White,       // light
        Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

    myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(
        Label.TextColorProperty,
        Colors.Black,       // light
        Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

    myButton.SetAppTheme(
        Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
        Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),  // light
        Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC")); // dark
}
```

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live update** | Values swap automatically when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a specific theme |
| **Scalable approach** | For larger apps, define colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them via `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of inline hex values |
